name: Valencia Street
vibe: The Mission's gentrification spine — restaurants, bookshops, bars, bike lane, taquerias fading to artisanal

name: Mission Street
vibe: Working-class corridor, pan-Asian restaurants, dollar stores, Muni 14, the real Mission

name: 24th Street
vibe: Southern Mission heart — tortillerias, carnicerias, indie cafes, community murals

name: Market Street
vibe: SF's main artery — Muni Metro, Castro to Ferry Building, bike lanes, Pride parade route

name: Castro Street
vibe: The Castro's vertical strip — bars, bookshops, Castro Theatre marquee, rainbow crosswalks

name: Haight Street
vibe: Head shops to vintage stores — tourist Haight blending into grittier Lower Haight bars

name: Fillmore Street
vibe: Jazz history, sushi row, boutiques — ties Pacific Heights to the Fillmore's legacy

name: Divisadero Street
vibe: Gentrifying corridor, brunch spots, barbershops, pan-Asian, NoPa heart

name: Chestnut Street
vibe: Marina's boutique strip — fitness, brunch, weekend shopping, young professionals

name: Union Street
vibe: Cow Hollow's upscale row — wine bars, antiques, restaurants, Victorians

name: Columbus Avenue
vibe: North Beach's diagonal — Italian restaurants, City Lights, Vesuvio, leads to Chinatown edge

name: Grant Avenue
vibe: Chinatown's tourist face — shops, dim sum, dragon gates — shifts to North Beach above Broadway

name: Clement Street
vibe: Inner Richmond's main street — Asian restaurants, Russian bakeries, produce shops, very local

name: Irving Street
vibe: Sunset's café row — N-Judah runs alongside, bookstores, tea shops, close to UCSF

name: Judah Street
vibe: N-Judah runs down the middle to Ocean Beach — surf shops, family restaurants, foghorns

name: Third Street
vibe: T-Third streetcar corridor — industrial becoming creative, Dogpatch craft breweries to Bayview

name: Embarcadero
vibe: Bay-facing boulevard — Ferry Building to AT&T Park, cyclists, joggers, bay air

name: The Great Highway
vibe: Ocean Beach edge road — closed to cars on weekends, surfers parking, foghorns, windswept

name: 19th Avenue
vibe: City spine through the avenues — mostly cars, connects GG Park to Daly City

name: Potrero Avenue
vibe: Hospital row (SF General), auto shops, transitional between SoMa and Potrero Hill
